response: Implemented in May 2018, the EU's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) mandates strict data handling standards for any organization processing EU residents' information, guaranteeing rights to access, erasure, and explicit consent alongside a 72-hour breach reporting requirement. Non-compliance can trigger fines up to 4% of global annual revenue or €20 million, leading to major penalties like €1.2 billion for Meta, €746 million for Amazon, and over €200 million for Google. While critics highlight disproportionate compliance burdens, inconsistent enforcement, and user frustration, the GDPR has successfully established data privacy as a fundamental right and set a global benchmark that has inspired similar legislation worldwide.
